summary.intent: Address three P2 Codex review findings on PR #697 in the date_picker state machine: stale focus intents, incorrect ICU field ordering for non-Gregorian/native-digit locales, and closed pickers reopening on stale SelectDate events.
summary.outcome: All three findings were fixed using TDD (red-green), the spec was synced to match, all CI gates passed (cargo xci-fast, 10 gates), and changes were committed and pushed to PR #697.

repo: CLAUDE.md requires loading the icu4x skill before editing any ICU4X-related code in ars-i18n.
The spec (spec/components/date-time/date-picker.md) contains mirrored pseudocode of the state machine and must be kept in sync whenever state machine logic changes.
cargo xci-fast is the pre-push CI profile and validates spec snippet compilation via spec-compile-snippets gate.
Proptests are marked #[ignore] for nightly but can be run explicitly to verify invariants hold.
Zero-warnings policy is enforced — clippy must be clean on all touched crates before pushing.
state_changed is true whenever a target is set on the engine, regardless of whether the value changed — it does not indicate a semantic state transition.

code.finding: open_request did not take ctx, so a vetoed controlled-open left requested_focus=Some(OpenFocus::None) permanently; subsequent programmatic opens inherited the stale marker and suppressed FocusCalendar. Fix: pass ctx and clear requested_focus on any no-op close.
SelectDate with close_on_select=false forced State::Open unconditionally, reopening already-closed pickers after outside-close/focus-out races. Fix: preserve state.clone() instead of forcing Open.

date_order probe used a plain locale, so fa-IR converted to Solar Hijri calendar (probe values no longer 11/22/2006) and emitted Persian digits the parser couldn't read, causing wrong fallback to DayMonthYear. Fix: force Gregorian calendar on probe locale and normalize digits before parsing.
Added pub(crate) with_gregorian_calendar() helper that sets the u-ca-gregory Unicode extension keyword on a Locale, enabling calendar-forced probing in date_order without changing the public API.
normalize_digits helper already exists and handles native-digit transliteration; it just wasn't being called in numeric_field_order_from_formatted before this fix.

workflow: Empirical probing via throwaway tests (then deleted) is the right approach before fixing ICU behavior — ICU calendar/digit output is non-obvious and locale-specific.
TDD red-green cycle was strictly followed: write failing test, confirm red, implement fix, confirm green, then clean up.
For state machine bugs, checking the engine's semantics (state_changed behavior) before writing assertions avoids false-red tests.
Spec sync should happen after all code tests are green, not before — the spec mirrors implementation and drifts otherwise.

friction: fa-IR's Persian calendar conversion destroyed the probe date values (11/22/2006 became ۱۳۸۵/۹/۱), requiring calendar forcing — this was not anticipated from reading the code alone and needed empirical discovery.
The correct value accessor on the test API (ctx.value.get()) wasn't obvious and required inspecting the Api struct after an initial wrong assertion attempt.
state_changed=true on a no-op close was surprising and required reading engine internals (ars-core/src/lib.rs) to understand the semantics before dropping the assertion.
open_items: Proptests are skipped by default (marked #[ignore] for nightly) — the date_picker proptest was run manually this session but is not part of the normal CI gate.
Finding #2's fix adds a gregorian-calendar forced probe path for both icu and js formatters, but the js path's behavior under non-Gregorian locales was not independently verified beyond the ICU path.
